fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need to build out this security header middleware engine we've been talking about. The basic idea is that you give it a config describing what protections you want on your HTTP responses, and it spits out the right headers automatically. No more hand-rolling these things and getting the formatting wrong.

A few things I know we need: there should be a 'batteries included' mode that just turns on everything sensible at once for teams that don't want to think about it. Individual headers should also be toggleable separately. Some of the headers have dependencies on each other — refer back to how we handled the cross-origin isolation stuff in the embedder policy discussion, the same gating logic applies here. We also need path-based rules so the data-clearing header only fires on specific routes like logout pages.

The engine reads a JSON blob on stdin and writes headers to stdout, one per line, sorted alphabetically. If something is misconfigured it should spit out a neutral error token — no stack traces, no runtime garbage, just clean error codes the frontend team can parse.

Edge cases to handle: what if someone passes in no config at all? What if they want certain paths completely ignored? The CSP stuff has some tricky quoting and deduplication rules too that we need to get right. Output should be totally deterministic.

Can someone scope this out? I think the public test cases folder has examples of expected outputs we can validate against.

trigger_keywords: default bundle headers list
what does preset default emit
batteries included mode output
recommended baseline headers
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The default preset emits exactly 11 headers in alphabetical order: Cache-Control: max-age=0,no-store | Content-Security-Policy: script-src 'self';object-src 'self';block-all-mixed-content;upgrade-insecure-requests; | Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp | Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin | Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin | Referrer-Policy: no-referrer | Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000;includeSubDomains | X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff | X-Frame-Options: deny | X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies: none | X-XSS-Protection: 0

trigger_keywords: cross-origin embedder dependency rule
embedder policy requires what companion
what happens if embedder policy is requested alone
cross origin isolation gating logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy may only be emitted when cross_origin_resource_policy is also present in the same configuration. If embedder policy is requested without resource policy, the engine must reject the configuration and output exactly: error=embedder_policy_requires_resource_policy\n — no headers are emitted.

trigger_keywords: no configuration error token
what error when with_config false
missing config error format
engine invoked without configuration
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When with_config is false (or the engine is invoked with no configuration), the output must be exactly: error=missing_configuration\n. No headers, no additional lines. The error token must be neutral — no runtime language artifacts.

trigger_keywords: output ordering rule
how are headers sorted in output
header line order determinism
alphabetical sort headers
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: All emitted headers must be sorted in ascending ordinal (lexicographic) order by header name, regardless of the order they were configured. This applies to every mode including the default preset and custom header combinations. When no headers are produced, the output is exactly: <no-headers>\n

trigger_keywords: cache control precedence rules
which cache directive wins
cache control value priority order
no-cache vs private vs must-revalidate
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Cache-Control value follows strict precedence: (1) if no_cache is true → emit 'no-cache'; (2) else if private is true → emit 'private'; (3) else if must_revalidate is true → emit 'must-revalidate'; (4) otherwise emit 'max-age=<n>' and if no_store is true append ',no-store'. Default values: private=false, max_age=0, no_cache=false, no_store=true, must_revalidate=false.

trigger_keywords: CSP keyword token quoting
how are CSP source tokens formatted
CSP directive uri vs keyword difference
content security policy token format rules
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: In CSP directives, tokens of type 'keyword' are wrapped in single quotes (e.g. 'self', 'unsafe-inline'), while tokens of type 'uri' are emitted unquoted (e.g. data:, https://cdn.example.com). Exactly one space separates tokens with no leading or trailing spaces. Each source list ends with exactly one semicolon. Duplicate URI tokens must not be repeated.

trigger_keywords: report only mode CSP requirement
what is required for report only header
missing report_uri error
CSP report only validation rule
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When content_security_policy.report_only is true, report_uri is required and must be non-empty. If report_uri is absent or empty in report-only mode, the engine rejects the configuration with: error=missing_required_value\nfield=report_uri\n. The emitted header name changes to Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only and the value ends with report-uri <uri>;

trigger_keywords: clear site data wildcard behavior
what does star directive do in clear-site-data
wildcard token collapses clear site data
clear site data default directives
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For Clear-Site-Data: if any token in the directives array is '*', the entire value collapses to "*" (a single quoted wildcard), ignoring all other tokens. Duplicate tokens are removed. When directives is omitted or empty, the default is ["cache","cookies","storage"]. Each token is emitted quoted and comma-separated with no spaces, e.g.: "cache","cookies","storage"

trigger_keywords: path scoped clear site data longest match
which path wins when multiple prefixes match
most specific path rule
clear site data path matching algorithm
hidden_constraints.oracle_response:
When multiple configured paths are prefixes of the request path, the most specific (longest) match wins. For example if /account and /account/logout are both configured and the request is /account/logout, the /account/logout entry is used. An empty path string is rejected with error=missing_required_value
field=path
. An empty directives array in add_paths is rejected with error=missing_required_value
field=directives
.

trigger_keywords: ignore paths behavior
what happens when request path is in ignore list
ignored path emits what
suppress all headers for certain routes
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When a request_path appears in the ignore_paths array, the engine emits no headers at all for that request, outputting: <no-headers>\n. Other paths not in the ignore list are processed normally with the full configured or preset header set.

trigger_keywords: allow-from directive format
x-frame-options allowfrom value format
domain field in frame options
framing allow from origin syntax
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When x_frame_options.directive is 'allowfrom', the emitted header value is: allow-from: (<domain>) — note the colon-space after 'allow-from' and the parentheses wrapping the domain. For example: X-Frame-Options: allow-from: (https://example.com)

trigger_keywords: expect ct value format
how is expect-ct header constructed
expect ct enforce and report uri ordering
certificate transparency header format
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Expect-CT value always starts with max-age=<n> (default 86400). If enforce is true, append ', enforce'. If report_uri is non-empty, append ', report-uri="<uri>"'. An empty report_uri string is silently ignored (no error). Example: max-age=86400, enforce, report-uri="https://ex.com/r"

trigger_keywords: when preset is used headers object ignored
preset overrides individual header config
preset and headers field interaction
default preset ignores custom headers
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When the 'preset' field is set to 'default', the headers object is completely ignored. The preset applies its own fixed opinionated configuration regardless of what is specified in the headers field. These are mutually exclusive modes.

trigger_keywords: legacy compat CSP header
x-content-security-policy mirror
legacy_compat field in CSP
old browser CSP compatibility header
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When content_security_policy.legacy_compat is true, the policy value is mirrored into an additional X-Content-Security-Policy header with the same value as the Content-Security-Policy header. Both headers appear in the output, sorted alphabetically among all other headers.

trigger_keywords: HSTS include subdomains default
strict transport security default values
what is default max age for HSTS
includeSubDomains default behavior
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Strict-Transport-Security defaults: max_age=31536000, include_subdomains=true. When include_subdomains is true the value is max-age=<n>;includeSubDomains (no space around semicolon). When include_subdomains is false the value is just max-age=<n>.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
single file or multiple files
project structure
directory layout
should I split into multiple files
monolithic or modular
how complex is the codebase
what files should I create
how to structure the implementation
should I separate business logic from IO
decouple core from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
SOLID principles
separation of concerns
adapter pattern
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the dispatcher work
how is the program invoked
stdin stdout interface
execution adapter
how to read input
how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

trigger_keywords: how to write test.sh
test harness scaffold
automated test script
how should test.sh work
cases-dir parameter
where does test output go
output file naming format
how are test outputs named
rcb_tests structure
test scaffold deliverable
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: do you have test cases
are there public test cases
what test cases are provided
can I see the test inputs
what features have test cases
show me the test cases
what test files exist
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

context_pointers.vague_reference: the same gating logic from the embedder policy discussion
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Feature 12 in start.md: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy has a hard dependency on cross_origin_resource_policy being present in the same configuration; absence triggers error=embedder_policy_requires_resource_policy

context_pointers.vague_reference: the data-clearing header only fires on specific routes like logout pages
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Feature 16 in start.md: clear_site_data_paths and clear_site_data_add_paths configuration shapes under headers, with longest-prefix path matching logic

context_pointers.vague_reference: neutral error token — no stack traces, no runtime garbage
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The Errors section of the Architecture constraints in start.md: only four specific error= tokens are permitted in output; no host-language type names, stack traces or runtime message fragments

context_pointers.vague_reference: the CSP stuff has some tricky quoting and deduplication rules
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Feature 14 in start.md: keyword tokens wrapped in single quotes vs uri tokens unquoted, one-space separation, single trailing semicolon per directive, duplicate URI removal

context_pointers.vague_reference: public test cases folder has examples of expected outputs
context_pointers.actual_meaning: rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ directory containing feature1 through feature17 JSON files with input/expected_output pairs for all engine behaviors

oracle_data.fallback_response: That detail isn't something I have clear visibility on from the business side — you'd want to dig into the test cases in the public_test_cases folder or the spec doc to confirm the exact behavior expected there.

feature10_cross_origin_resource_policy.j.description: The Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header carries the selected policy token (defaulting to same-origin) and is absent when the feature is not enabled.

feature11_cross_origin_opener_policy.jso.description: The Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header carries the selected policy token (defaulting to same-origin) and is absent when the feature is not enabled.

feature12_cross_origin_embedder_policy.j.description: The Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header may only be emitted alongside an enabled Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy. With the resource policy present both headers are emitted; requesting the embedder policy alone is rejected as an invalid configuration; the header is absent when not enabled.

feature13_content_security_policy.json.description: The Content-Security-Policy family: a plain enabled policy emits the block/upgrade directives; a sandbox prepends a sandbox directive; legacy-compatibility mode mirrors the policy into the X-Content-Security-Policy header; report-only mode emits a separate report-only header carrying a report-uri; and no header is emitted when the policy is not enabled.

feature14_csp_directive_formatting.json.description: Content-Security-Policy directive formatting rules: keyword tokens are wrapped in single quotes, URI tokens are left unquoted, exactly one space separates tokens (no leading, trailing or double spaces), each source list ends with a single semicolon, and a repeated URI is not duplicated.

feature15_clear_site_data_global.json.description: When Clear-Site-Data is enabled for all responses the header lists each requested data type as a quoted token; a wildcard collapses the whole value to "*" and takes precedence over any other token; duplicate tokens are removed; and the header is absent when not enabled.

feature16_clear_site_data_paths.json.description: Path-scoped Clear-Site-Data emits the header only for requests whose path matches a configured entry; when several configured paths are prefixes of the request the most specific (longest) match wins; and configuring an entry with an empty path or an empty token list is rejected as an invalid configuration.

feature17_middleware_contract.json.description: Operational request-handling contract: invoking the engine without any configuration is rejected; and when an ignore list is in effect the engine emits no headers for an ignored request path while still applying the full bundle to a non-ignored path.

feature1_default_bundle.json.description: Applying the recommended default security-header bundle to a normal request emits the complete, opinionated set of OWASP response headers with their canonical wire values.

cases.expected_output:
Cache-Control: max-age=0,no-store
Content-Security-Policy: script-src 'self';object-src 'self';block-all-mixed-content;upgrade-insecure-requests;
Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp
Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin
Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin
Referrer-Policy: no-referrer
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000;includeSubDomains
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
X-Frame-Options: deny
X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies: none
X-XSS-Protection: 0

feature2_strict_transport_security.json.description: The Strict-Transport-Security header is emitted only when HSTS is enabled; its value encodes the configured max-age and whether sub-domains are included.

feature3_x_frame_options.json.description: The X-Frame-Options header reflects the chosen framing directive; the allow-from directive additionally carries the permitted origin, and the header is absent when the feature is not enabled.

feature4_content_type_options.json.description: The X-Content-Type-Options header emits the fixed nosniff token when enabled and is absent otherwise.

feature5_xss_protection.json.description: The X-XSS-Protection header emits the value 0 (disabling the legacy browser auditor, per current guidance) when enabled and is absent otherwise.

feature6_referrer_policy.json.description: The Referrer-Policy header carries the selected policy token (defaulting to no-referrer) and is absent when the feature is not enabled.

feature7_permitted_cross_domain_policies.description: The X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies header carries the selected policy token (defaulting to none) and is absent when the feature is not enabled.

feature8_cache_control.json.description: The Cache-Control header value is produced by a strict precedence among the requested directives: no-cache wins over private, which wins over must-revalidate, which wins over the max-age/no-store pair.

feature9_expect_ct.json.description: The Expect-CT header always carries max-age, optionally appends enforce, and appends a quoted report-uri only when one is supplied; the header is absent when the feature is not enabled.
